steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to contrast the two moons’ orbits — that means highlighting a difference between them. Scan the notes for any point where Callisto and Adrastea differ: Callisto orbits in 16.69 Earth days; Adrastea orbits in just 0.30 Earth days. That enormous gap in orbital period is the only contrast the notes actually support.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Adrastea completes its orbit of Jupiter many times faster than Callisto does.': Directly expresses the contrast between 0.30 and 16.69 days. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Jupiter's moons, specifically Callisto and Adrastea, orbit their planet in the same way the planets orbit the Sun.': Describes a similarity (both follow Kepler's laws), not a contrast. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Kepler's three laws of planetary motion describe the orbits of planets and natural satellites, including those of Callisto and Adrastea.': Also a similarity — both moons follow the same laws. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Callisto's orbit is elliptical...while Adrastea's orbit is circular...': Claims Adrastea's orbit is circular, but the notes say Kepler's laws (which describe elliptical orbits) apply to all moons — this is unsupported. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
